subquestions: If a single event triggers claims under both flood and storm sections, how many excesses apply?

initial_drafts.Does the policy cover flood?: The policy does not automatically provide cover for loss or damage caused by Flood (p. 7). However, there is an option to apply for cover for Flood under the Commercial Property Section, and if agreed, it will be shown on the Schedule (p. 7). When 'Flood' cover is shown in the current Schedule as covered, We will cover loss of or Damage to Property Insured caused by Flood (p. 30).
initial_drafts.If a single event triggers claims under: According to Document > 15. Payment of Excess (p. 24), if a single event results in claiming under more than one Section of the Policy, then only the single highest Excess will apply to all claims that arise out of the one event.

final_drafts.Does the policy cover flood?: The policy does not automatically provide cover for loss or damage caused by Flood (p. 7). However, there is an option to apply for cover for Flood under the Commercial Property Section, and if agreed, it will be shown on the Schedule (p. 7). When 'Flood' cover is shown in the current Schedule as covered, We will cover loss of or Damage to Property Insured caused by Flood (p. 30).
final_drafts.If a single event triggers claims under:
According to Document > 15. Payment of Excess (p. 24), if a single event results in claiming under more than one Section of the Policy, then only the single highest Excess will apply to all claims that arise out of the one event. However, it does not specify how many excesses apply in this scenario.

A separate excerpt Document > 15. Payment of Excess (p. 24) states: "Only one Excess will apply to all Damage resulting from Flood, earthquake, subterranean fire, volcanic eruption, bushfire, storm, hail or rainwater that is continuous, is due to the same general conditions, and occurs within a 72-hour period from the first happening of Damage." This excerpt implies that in the case of flood and storm sections, only one excess applies.

runs.final_brief:
**Policy Coverage Brief**

The policy does not automatically provide cover for loss or damage caused by Flood (p. 7), but there is an option to apply for cover under the Commercial Property Section, which would be shown on the Schedule (p. 7). If 'Flood' cover is shown as covered in the current Schedule, We will cover loss of or Damage to Property Insured caused by Flood (p. 30).

If a single event triggers claims under both flood and storm sections, only one excess applies (Document > 15. Payment of Excess, p. 24). This is because the policy states that "Only one Excess will apply to all Damage resulting from...storm..." (Document > 15. Payment of Excess, p. 24), implying that in the case of flood and storm sections, only one excess applies.

**Sources:**

p. 7
p. 30
Document > 15. Payment of Excess, p. 24

This is general information about the PDS, not personal financial advice.

baseline.answer:
Yes, the policy covers flood (p. 24).

If a single event causes both flood and storm damage, only one Excess will apply to all Damage resulting from Flood, earthquake, subterranean fire, volcanic eruption, bushfire, storm, hail or rainwater that is continuous, is due to the same general conditions, and occurs within a 72-hour period from the first happening of Damage (p. 24).

Sources:
(p. 24)

initial_drafts.What does Excess mean?: Excess means either the amount of money specified in the Schedule or otherwise stated in the Policy for each applicable Section that You must contribute for all claims arising out of one event, or the period of time specified in the Schedule or otherwise stated in the Policy for which no payment will be made by Us. (p. 16)
initial_drafts.Which excess applies when one event trig: According to Document > 15. Payment of Excess (p. 24), if a single event results in You claiming under more than one Section of Your Policy, then only the single highest Excess will apply to all claims that arise out of the one event.

final_drafts.What does Excess mean?: Excess means either the amount of money specified in the Schedule or otherwise stated in the Policy for each applicable Section that You must contribute for all claims arising out of one event, or the period of time specified in the Schedule or otherwise stated in the Policy for which no payment will be made by Us. (p. 16)
final_drafts.Which excess applies when one event trig: According to Document > 15. Payment of Excess (p. 16), if a single event results in You claiming under more than one Section of Your Policy, then only the single highest Excess will apply to all claims that arise out of the one event.

runs.final_brief:
Here is the combined brief:

Excess refers to either the amount of money specified in the Schedule or otherwise stated in the Policy for each applicable Section that You must contribute for all claims arising out of one event, or the period of time specified in the Schedule or otherwise stated in the Policy for which no payment will be made by Us (p. 16).

When one event triggers claims under more than one section, only the single highest Excess applies to all claims that arise out of the one event (Document > 15. Payment of Excess, p. 16).

Sources:
(p. 16)

This is general information about the PDS, not personal financial advice.

initial_drafts.Are there any exclusions or limitations:
Yes, there are several exclusions and limitations mentioned in the excerpts:

* Exclusion of loss or damage caused by Flood is mentioned on p. 7.
* Electronic Data exclusion is applicable to Machinery Breakdown Section and Electronic Equipment Section (p. 19).
* The Policy does not cover loss, destruction, liability or Damage directly or indirectly, caused by, contributed to by or arising from the acquisition of companies (p. 6).
* The Policy does not provide any cover at an Unoccupied Building after it has been left vacant for more than 60 consecutive days, except in certain circumstances (p. 7).

final_drafts.Are there any exclusions or limitations:
Yes, there are several exclusions and limitations mentioned:

* The Policy does not cover loss or damage caused by Flood (p. 7).
* Electronic Data exclusion is applicable to Machinery Breakdown Section and Electronic Equipment Section (pp. 19).
* The Policy does not provide any cover at an Unoccupied Building after it has been left vacant for more than 60 consecutive days, except in certain circumstances (p. 7).

runs.final_brief:
The sections insured under this policy include:

* This document, which sets out the standard terms of Your cover and its limitations
* Your current Schedule issued by Us
* Any other change to the terms of the Policy otherwise advised by Us in writing (such as an Endorsement or Supplementary PDS) before You enter into the Policy or where required or permitted by law

Each Section may also contain definitions relevant to that Section of the Policy.

However, there are several exclusions and limitations to these sections, including:

* Loss or damage caused by Flood
* Electronic Data exclusion applicable to Machinery Breakdown Section and Electronic Equipment Section
* No cover at an Unoccupied Building after it has been left vacant for more than 60 consecutive days, except in certain circumstances.

Sources:
pp. 7, 9, 10, 11, 19

This is general information about the PDS, not personal financial advice.
